container_types.description: Standard 20ft general-purpose dry container suitable for most FCL shipments, ideal for loads around 28–30 m³.

help_articles.title: Lithium Batteries: General Shipping Requirements
help_articles.body:
This article provides an overview of how to ship lithium batteries safely and in compliance with international regulations.

## 1. Types of lithium batteries
We distinguish between:
- Lithium-ion (rechargeable)
- Lithium metal (non-rechargeable)
- Equipment containing lithium batteries
- Batteries packed with or packed separately from equipment

## 2. Transport modes
Lithium batteries can move by air, ocean, and ground. Each mode has its own rules (IATA DGR for air, IMDG Code for ocean, ADR/49 CFR for road/rail).

## 3. Key requirements
- Correct UN number (e.g., UN3480, UN3481, UN3090, UN3091)
- Proper packaging that protects against short circuits
- State of charge (SoC) limits for air transport
- Performance-tested UN packaging for fully regulated shipments
- Appropriate labels and marks (lithium battery mark, Class 9 hazard label, cargo aircraft only label when applicable)

## 4. Documentation
You may need:
- Shipper's Declaration for Dangerous Goods (air)
- Dangerous goods declaration (ocean)
- Safety data sheet (SDS)

## 5. Restrictions
Some origins/destinations, airlines, and carriers may:
- Prohibit standalone lithium batteries (UN3480/UN3090) by air
- Limit quantities per package
- Require pre-approval for battery-powered vehicles and large battery packs

Still need help? Scroll to the bottom of this page and click **"Contact us"** to send your dangerous goods question to our support team.

help_articles.title: Lithium Batteries – Air Shipping to Canada
help_articles.body:
This article explains the main rules for shipping lithium batteries by air from the United States or other countries to Canada.

## 1. Applicable regulations
Shipments of lithium batteries to Canada by air must comply with:
- IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR)
- ICAO Technical Instructions
- Any additional carrier and airport restrictions

## 2. What can be shipped
In general, you may ship:
- Lithium-ion batteries contained in or packed with equipment
- Small lithium-ion and lithium metal cells/batteries within Section II thresholds (when carriers still accept them)
- Fully regulated Class 9 lithium batteries when properly declared and packaged

Standalone lithium batteries (UN3480/UN3090) may be restricted or prohibited on some routes or airlines. Always check carrier acceptance.

## 3. Packaging and labelling
- Protect terminals from short circuits (insulation, individual inner packaging)
- Use strong outer packaging capable of withstanding normal transport conditions
- Apply the lithium battery mark when required (with phone number and UN number)
- Apply Class 9 lithium battery hazard labels when shipping fully regulated quantities
- Use **Cargo Aircraft Only** labels if required by IATA packing instructions

## 4. Documentation
Depending on the configuration and quantity, you may need:
- Shipper's Declaration for Dangerous Goods
- Air waybill with proper dangerous goods entries
- Safety data sheet (SDS) for the battery

## 5. Destination-specific considerations in Canada
Some Canadian airports, airline partners, and last-mile carriers have stricter rules for lithium batteries, especially for:
- High-capacity battery packs
- Damaged or recalled batteries (these are generally prohibited)

We recommend confirming acceptance for routes such as **US to Toronto (YYZ)**, **US to Vancouver (YVR)**, or **US to Montreal (YUL)** before booking.

## 6. Getting assistance
If you are unsure whether your lithium battery shipment to Canada can move via air service:
1. Prepare the SDS and basic details (UN number, Wh rating, number of cells/batteries).
2. Contact our dangerous goods team using the **"Contact us"** button at the bottom of this article.
3. In the contact form, select **Dangerous Goods** as the topic and provide route details (for example, *US to Toronto, Canada via air*).

Our specialists will review your information and confirm service availability and packing/documentation requirements.

help_articles.title: How to Declare Dangerous Goods on Your Shipment
help_articles.body:
Correctly declaring dangerous goods helps prevent delays, surcharges, and safety issues.

## 1. Information you need
Before you create a dangerous goods shipment, collect:
- UN number and proper shipping name
- Class, division, and packing group
- Quantity and packing type
- Safety data sheet (SDS)

## 2. During quote or booking
In the **Ship & Quote** form:
1. Select your service type (air, ocean, ground).
2. In the *Cargo type* or *Special handling* section, enable **Dangerous goods**.
3. Enter the UN number, class, and packing group when prompted.

## 3. Documentation
Depending on mode and configuration, you may be asked to upload or provide:
- Shipper's Declaration for Dangerous Goods
- SDS
- Additional permits or approvals for certain countries

## 4. Review by our team
High-risk or restricted items may require manual review. If additional information is needed, our dangerous goods specialists will contact you using the details provided with your booking.

For lithium batteries and other sensitive items, also see our articles on lithium battery shipping requirements.

If you still have questions, use the **"Contact us"** link at the bottom of this article and choose **Dangerous Goods** as your topic.

help_articles.title: Packing and Labelling Guidelines for Dangerous Goods
help_articles.body:
Proper packing and labelling are essential for any dangerous goods shipment.

## 1. General packing principles
- Use UN performance-tested packaging when required
- Prevent movement of inner receptacles inside outer packaging
- Protect closures, valves, and vents from damage
- Prevent leaks and spills under normal transport conditions

## 2. Labelling and marking
- Apply the correct hazard labels for the class/division
- Apply orientation arrows when needed
- For lithium batteries, apply the lithium battery mark and any required Class 9 or Cargo Aircraft Only labels
- Ensure labels are:
  - On a contrasting background
  - Not covered by tape or straps
  - Visible on at least one side of the package

## 3. Documentation
Always ensure the details on your labels, marks, and documentation are consistent (UN number, proper shipping name, and class).

For specific lithium battery rules and examples, see our lithium battery articles in the Dangerous Goods category.

If you need help verifying your packing design, click **"Contact us"** below and attach photos or drawings of your packaging.

help_articles.title: How to Get a Freight Quote Online
help_articles.body:
You can get instant quotes for air, ocean, and ground shipments directly from our website.

## 1. Open the Ship & Quote form
1. From the header, click **Ship & Quote**.
2. Choose the appropriate service type: Air, Ocean, Ground, or Refrigerated.

## 2. Enter route and shipment details
- Origin and destination (city, postal code, or port)
- Weight and volume or package dimensions
- Cargo type (general, dangerous goods, refrigerated, etc.)

## 3. View and sort available services
After clicking **Get Rates** or **Show Rates**:
- Use filters for transit time (e.g., 3 days or less)
- Sort by **Price: Low to High** or **Earliest delivery**

## 4. Saving a quote
In the results list, click **Save quote** to store a quote in your **Saved Quotes** page for later booking.

For special cargo (like lithium batteries or temperature-controlled freight), make sure you enable the appropriate cargo options so we can display only compatible services.

help_articles.title: Understanding Air Freight Transit Times
help_articles.body:
Air freight services on our platform show an **estimated transit time** in days.

## 1. What transit time includes
The displayed days typically cover:
- Airport-to-airport transport
- Standard handling at origin and destination terminals

## 2. Examples
- Los Angeles (LAX) to Tokyo (NRT/HND): commonly 3–5 days depending on service
- New York (JFK) to London (LHR): commonly 2–4 days

## 3. Choosing the right service
When you view air freight quotes:
1. Click **Ship & Quote** and select **Air Freight**.
2. Enter your route (for example, *Los Angeles, USA* to *Tokyo, Japan*), weight, and volume.
3. Click **Show Rates** and then sort by **Price: Low to High**.
4. Check the *Estimated transit time* column and pick a service that meets your delivery requirement (e.g., **5 days or less**).

Keep in mind that customs clearance or security inspections can add extra time for some shipments.

help_articles.title: Choosing an FCL Container Size for Ocean Freight
help_articles.body:
When you request an **Ocean Freight – Full Container Load (FCL)** quote, you can compare several container sizes.

## 1. Common container types
Examples of standard dry containers:
- 20ft Standard Dry (approx. 33 m³)
- 40ft Standard Dry (approx. 68 m³)
- 40ft High Cube (approx. 76 m³)
- 45ft High Cube (approx. 85 m³)

## 2. Matching your cargo volume
In the quote form:
1. Select **Ocean Freight** and **Full Container Load (FCL)**.
2. Enter your route, for example *Shanghai, China* to *Los Angeles, USA*.
3. Enter your estimated volume (e.g., **28 m³**).
4. Click **Show Container Options**.

The system will display each container type with its capacity and price. Choose the **cheapest container** whose capacity is **greater than or equal to your volume** (e.g., any container with capacity ≥ 28 m³ and price < your budget).

## 3. Other considerations
- Maximum gross weight
- High cube requirements for tall cargo
- Carrier availability on your lane

help_articles.title: Booking Refrigerated Shipments Within Europe
help_articles.body:
You can book temperature-controlled services for lanes within Europe, such as **Paris to Rome**.

## 1. Enabling refrigerated service
1. Click **Ship & Quote**.
2. Choose **Freight shipment** and enter origin and destination (e.g., *Paris, France* to *Rome, Italy*).
3. Enter weight and volume.
4. In *Cargo type* or *Special handling*, select **Refrigerated** or **Temperature-controlled**.

## 2. Viewing suitable services
After you click **Show Rates**:
- Apply any **Refrigerated** filter to show only temperature-controlled options.
- Sort by **Earliest delivery** or **Transit time: Shortest first**.

## 3. Price considerations
When comparing refrigerated services:
- Look for options under your maximum budget (for example, **under USD 2,500**).
- From the qualifying services, choose the one that appears first in the **earliest delivery** sort order.

Refrigerated services may have cut-off times and limited departure days. Book as early as possible to secure capacity.

help_articles.title: Domestic Ground Services in the United States
help_articles.body:
Our platform offers several domestic ground services across the United States.

## 1. Typical use cases
- Warehouse to store deliveries
- Multi-package shipments
- Palletized LTL or full truckload moves

## 2. Example: Chicago to New York
To book a domestic ground shipment from **Chicago** to **New York**:
1. Click **Schedule Pickup**.
2. Enter the pickup address (for example, *123 Warehouse Ave, Chicago, IL 60601*).
3. Enter the delivery address (for example, *789 Retail Rd, New York, NY 10001*).
4. Provide shipment details (number of boxes, total weight).
5. Choose a pickup date and time window (e.g., **2:00 PM – 5:00 PM**).
6. Click **See Services**, then filter by **3 days or less** and sort by **Price: Low to High**.

Select a service with a total price at or below your budget and proceed to booking.

help_articles.title: How to Change Your Delivery Address After Shipment
help_articles.body:
If your shipment is already in transit, you may still be able to change the delivery address.

## 1. Open the tracking page
1. In the header, click **Track Shipment**.
2. Enter your tracking number and click **Track**.

## 2. Manage delivery options
On the tracking details page:
1. Click **Manage Delivery** or **Change Delivery**.
2. In the address section, update the street information while keeping the city and postal code correct. For example, change the street to **"456 New Street"** while keeping the city as **Seattle** and the same ZIP code.

## 3. Adjust the delivery date
1. Open the calendar widget in the delivery date field.
2. Select a new date (for example, **3 days later** than the originally shown delivery date).

## 4. Confirm your changes
1. Click **Save changes** or **Update delivery**.
2. If prompted, confirm again with **"Yes, update"**.

Some services or shipment stages may not allow address changes. If the option is not available, contact support using the **Contact** page.

help_articles.title: Managing Delivery Dates and Time Windows
help_articles.body:
Many services allow you to select or modify your delivery date and time window.

## 1. At booking time
When creating a shipment or scheduling a pickup:
- Choose your preferred delivery time commitment when available (e.g., **by 12:00 PM**, **end of day**).
- For ground services, some lanes offer **morning delivery** or **by noon** options.

## 2. After booking
To review or adjust delivery timing:
1. Go to **Track Shipment**.
2. Enter your tracking number and open the shipment details.
3. If supported by the service, click **Manage Delivery** to request a new date.

Note: Changes may affect the price or transit time and are subject to carrier approval.

help_articles.title: Understanding Shipment Tracking Statuses
help_articles.body:
Tracking events show the progress of your shipment from pickup to delivery.

## 1. Common statuses
- **Label created**: Shipment information received, waiting for pickup.
- **Picked up**: Carrier has collected the shipment.
- **In transit**: Shipment is moving through the network.
- **Out for delivery**: Shipment is on a vehicle for final delivery.
- **Delivered**: Shipment has been delivered.

## 2. Exception statuses
- **Delay – weather**
- **Delay – customs**
- **Address issue** (you may need to correct or confirm the address)

If you see an **Address issue** or need to redirect a shipment within the same city, use the **Manage Delivery** link on the tracking details page or contact support.

help_articles.title: Using the Address Book and Setting a Default Pickup Location
help_articles.body:
Your **Address Book** lets you store frequently used locations such as warehouses and client stores.

## 1. Opening the address book
1. Click **Address Book** in the header or footer.
2. Sign in if prompted.

## 2. Adding a new address
1. Click **Add New Address**.
2. Enter a label such as **"Main Warehouse"** or **"Client Store"**.
3. Fill in the street, city, state, postal code, and country (for example, *123 Warehouse Ave, Chicago, IL 60601* or *789 Retail Rd, New York, NY 10001*).
4. Optionally add contact, phone, and notes.
5. Save the address.

## 3. Setting the default pickup location
1. In the address list, find your main pickup address (for example, **"Main Warehouse"** in Chicago).
2. Click **Set as default pickup**.
3. Confirm in the dialog if prompted.

The default pickup location will be pre-filled in forms such as **Schedule Pickup** and **Ship & Quote**.

help_articles.title: Working with Saved Quotes
help_articles.body:
Saved quotes allow you to compare options and book later without re-entering shipment details.

## 1. Saving a quote
After you run a search in **Ship & Quote** and see the list of services:
1. Sort and filter to find the option you like.
2. Click **Save quote** or **Add to saved quotes** next to that service.

## 2. Viewing saved quotes
1. Click **Saved Quotes** in the header.
2. Review your stored quotes, including route, service type, price, and validity period.

## 3. Booking from a saved quote
If the quote is still valid:
1. Open the quote from the **Saved Quotes** page.
2. Click **Book now** to convert it into a shipment.

Saved quotes are especially useful when comparing multiple air freight options (for example, from **Los Angeles to Tokyo** for a 120 kg shipment) or when you need internal approval before booking.

help_articles.title: Setting Up Recurring Weekly Pickups
help_articles.body:
If you ship regularly from the same address, recurring pickups can save time.

## 1. Open the recurring pickups page
1. Click **Recurring Pickups** in the header.
2. Go to the **Recurring Pickups** or **Regular Shipments** tab if necessary.

## 2. Configure your schedule
1. Enter the pickup address (for example, *500 Logistics Blvd, Atlanta, GA 30301*).
2. Set the **frequency** to **Weekly**.
3. Select **Tuesday** as the pickup day.
4. Choose a pickup time or window (e.g., **3:00 PM** or **3:00 PM – 5:00 PM**).
5. Set the start date to the first upcoming Tuesday of next month.
6. Set the end date to exactly **3 months** after the start date.
7. Choose **Standard Freight** as the service type.

## 3. Save the schedule
Click **Create Schedule** or **Save Recurring Pickup**. Your weekly pickup will then appear in your list of recurring schedules and will be used as the default for new bookings created from that schedule.
